rulings.Case Title: IN RE APPLICATION OF: KRYSTAL MICHELLE STEVENS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 2029.600
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, November 15, 2024, Line 1, PETITIONERS KRYSTAL STEVENS INDIVIDUALLY AND AS REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF ELIJAH ALEXANDER AULD, DECEASED, JOHN AULD, and JOSEPH STEVENS' Petition Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 2029.600. Petitioners' unopposed Petition Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2029.600 is granted. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work. =(302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL I. MINYARD JR VS. SCAFFOLD SOLUTIONS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, November 15, 2024, Line 12, DEFENDANT SKYLINE COMMERCIAL INTERIORS, INC. DBA SKYLINE CONSTRUCTION'SMOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication on cause of action one [negligence] is denied. There is a triable issue of fact whether defendant supplied defective scaffolding to plaintiff that affirmatively contributed to his injury. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work. =(302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: GENOA MARTELL VS. CONTEXTLOGIC, INC. (DBA "WISH)", A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, November 15, 2024, Line 16, DEFENDANTS WISH US HOLDINGS LLC, AND QOO10PTE LTD'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The Wahl declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" in compliance with CCP 430.41. The parties are ordered to comply with the code. The response to the first amended complaint is now due by December 20, 2024. Additionally, moving party is to comply with Local Rule 2.7 (courtesy copies) should it re-file the demurrer. =(302/RCE)
